evals.name: resist-ceremony-on-trivial-change
evals.prompt: the footer on our marketing site says Copyright 2024, needs to say 2026. also the link to the careers page 404s, should point to /careers not /career. can you sort that out
evals.expected_output: Recognizes this is far below the threshold where spec-driven ceremony pays off. Makes the two fixes (or explains exactly what to change). Produces NO specification, plan, or task artifact and does not walk the user through a multi-step workflow. If it mentions the methodology at all, it is one sentence explaining why the ceremony is not warranted.
assertions: Does not create or propose creating a spec.md, plan.md, or tasks.md
Does not enumerate a multi-step spec-driven workflow for the user to follow
Addresses both requested changes: the year and the careers link path
Any mention of spec-driven development is at most one sentence of justification, not a pitch
Does not offer the full workflow as an alternative the user could choose

evals.name: route-large-feature-to-full-flow
evals.prompt: we're adding multi-tenant support to our billing service. right now every table just has a customer_id and the API assumes one org per account. we need real tenant isolation, per-tenant rate limits, and an admin view that can impersonate a tenant for support. i have no idea where to start and my lead wants something written down before i touch code. how should i approach this?
evals.expected_output: Routes to the full workflow. Names the steps in dependency order and says which artifact each produces. States the specific signal that decided the classification (touches permissions and data isolation; multiple subsystems; user cannot state acceptance conditions; user explicitly needs something written down). Does not immediately start writing code or hand back generic architecture advice.
assertions: Recommends the full spec-driven workflow rather than starting implementation
Names workflow steps in an order that respects their prerequisites (specification before plan, plan before tasks, tasks before implementation)
States which artifact each named step produces
Names at least one concrete signal that justified the full band, drawn from the request itself
Gives exactly one recommendation rather than a menu of options
The actionable recommendation is present in the first response without a required follow-up

evals.name: diagnose-broken-mid-flow-state
evals.prompt: ok i'm confused. i ran the specify step then went straight to generating tasks and it complained. then i edited spec.md by hand to add two new requirements but plan.md still describes the old design. tasks.md has like 40 items half of which don't make sense anymore. what's the actual right way out of this without throwing everything away
evals.expected_output: Correctly separates two distinct problems: (a) generating tasks requires the plan to exist first, which is why it complained; (b) the hand-edit to the specification created drift, so the plan and task list are now stale relative to it. Prescribes a concrete recovery order (regenerate the plan from the updated spec, then regenerate tasks) and names a cross-artifact consistency check. Does not tell the user to start over.
assertions: Identifies the prerequisite failure as a separate problem from the drift
Explains that the task step refused because the plan artifact did not exist
Identifies which artifacts are stale relative to the hand-edited specification
Prescribes an explicit regeneration order rather than a vague suggestion to re-sync
Does not recommend discarding existing work or starting the feature over
Diagnoses the cause before prescribing the repair
